decisions.rationale: The gap consists of a comma and a space, which serves as a grammatical separator between the scope claim and the causal claim. It does not contain any assertive meaning.
The gap consists of a comma and a space, which grammatically separates a proposition from a limiting condition. It has no independent assertive content.
The gap consists only of a period, which is sentence-terminating punctuation and carries no assertive meaning.
The gap consists of a comma and a space, which grammatically separates the scope claim from the main prediction claim. It has no independent assertive content.
The gap consists of a comma and a space, which grammatically separates a prediction claim from a limiting condition. It has no independent assertive content.
The gap consists only of a period, which is sentence-terminating punctuation and carries no assertive meaning.
The gap consists of a semicolon and a space, which serves as a grammatical separator between two independent clauses that are both captured as claims. It has no independent assertive content.
The gap consists only of a period, which is sentence-terminating punctuation and carries no assertive meaning.
The gap consists of the word "and" with surrounding spaces, which functions as a non-assertive conjunction linking two independent clauses that are both captured as claims.
The gap consists only of a period, which is sentence-terminating punctuation and carries no assertive meaning.
The gap consists of the word "or" with surrounding spaces, which functions as a non-assertive conjunction linking two independent clauses that are both captured as claims.
The gap consists of a comma and a space, which grammatically separates a falsification claim from a limiting condition. It has no independent assertive content.
The gap consists only of a period, which is sentence-terminating punctuation and carries no assertive meaning.
The gap consists of a comma and the word "or", which functions as a non-assertive conjunction linking two independent clauses that are both captured as claims.
The gap consists only of a period, which is sentence-terminating punctuation and carries no assertive meaning.
